relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jacob dwelled in the land of his father’s sojournings.
Joseph entered the story as a seventeen-year-old shepherd with a troubling report.
Israel loved Joseph with visible favoritism and clothed him in a special tunic.
His brothers saw the uneven love and could not speak peace to him.
Joseph dreamed, and the dream deepened their hatred.
He asked them to hear the dream.
In the field, his sheaf rose while theirs bowed.
They heard rule in the dream and hated him more.
A second dream lifted the conflict into sun, moon, and stars.
Jacob rebuked him, hearing the whole household bowing.
The brothers envied him, but Jacob guarded the matter inside.
The brothers went to shepherd in Shechem.
Joseph answered the sending with presence: Here I am.
He was sent to seek the peace of brothers and flock.
A man found him wandering and asked what he sought.
Joseph said he was seeking his brothers.
He followed them to Dothan.
They saw him from afar and plotted before he arrived.
They named him master of dreams.
They planned to erase the dreamer and test the dream.
Reuben tried to rescue his life.
He urged the pit instead of bloodshed, hoping to return Joseph to his father.
They stripped Joseph of the tunic of favor.
They threw him into an empty, waterless pit.
They sat to eat while a caravan moved toward Egypt.
Judah asked what gain there was in killing and hiding blood.
He proposed selling their brother, their own flesh.
Joseph was drawn from the pit, sold for silver, and carried toward Egypt.
Reuben returned to emptiness and tore his garments.
He cried that the boy was gone.
The tunic was dipped in blood for a deception.
They asked their father to recognize what they had prepared.
Jacob recognized the tunic and believed Joseph torn away.
He tore his garments and entered long mourning.
He refused comfort and imagined going down to Sheol mourning his son.
Joseph was sold in Egypt into Potiphar’s house.
